name: Planet Labs PBC

profile.description: Planet Labs PBC operates the world's largest commercial Earth observation satellite constellation with 200+ satellites, providing daily global imaging data to governments, agriculture, forestry, defense, and financial intelligence customers. A Public Benefit Corporation, Planet achieved its first-ever full-year adjusted EBITDA and free cash flow profitability in FY2026 (ended January 2026) with record revenue of $307.7M and $900M backlog. Founded by former NASA scientists, headquartered in San Francisco. Market cap approximately $18.2B.

innovation.label: STRONG
innovation.analysis: Track Record (2/3): Planet achieved a fundamental business inflection in FY2026 — first full-year adjusted EBITDA profitability ($15.5M), first full-year free cash flow profitability ($53M), and 26% revenue growth to a record $307.7M [1]. Q4 FY2026 revenue surged 41% YoY to $86.8M, and Defense & Intelligence revenue grew over 70% YoY [1]. The company has successfully transformed from satellite hardware builder to a data-intelligence platform. AI Deployment (2/2): CEO Will Marshall declared '2026 is the year AI for Planet,' and the company is shipping AI-powered analytics including a chat interface to satellite imagery that can describe, search, plot charts, and do 'deep research' reports with access to 9 years of daily global Earth scans [2]. The NVIDIA collaboration accelerates GPU-powered imagery processing. These are customer-facing AI products generating revenue. Market Share (2/2): Planet operates the world's largest commercial Earth observation constellation, providing unmatched daily global coverage [1]. The company leads the EO market with a unique combination of coverage, revisit rate, and historical data archive that no competitor can replicate. Product Pipeline (1/2): Pelican next-gen satellite fleet expands capabilities, NGA and Navy contract wins demonstrate defense market penetration, and FY2027 guidance of $415-440M (+39% YoY) provides strong visibility with $900M backlog [1]. R&D Intensity (1/1): As a satellite and data platform company, Planet's R&D investment in constellation maintenance, data processing, and AI analytics represents high R&D intensity for its revenue base.

inspiration.label: DEVELOPING
inspiration.analysis: Mission Floor (3/4): Planet's PBC purpose — 'to accelerate humanity toward a more sustainable, secure, and prosperous world, by illuminating environmental and social change' — is specific, thoughtful, and uniquely encoded into the corporate structure as a fiduciary obligation [3]. The mission to 'image the world every day, and make change visible, accessible and actionable' connects satellite technology to environmental and social impact in a way that genuinely differentiates from peers. Founded by former NASA scientists who brought a public-service mindset to commercial space. Community Impact (1/2): Planet's Nonprofit Program provides satellite data to mission-driven NGOs and nonprofits at reduced rates, democratizing access to Earth observation for humanitarian applications including disaster response and climate monitoring [3]. Genuine but modest in scale relative to the company's commercial business. Employee Retention (1/2): The 800-person team has been built through 10+ years of funding rounds, SPAC listing, and financial turbulence under consistent co-founder leadership. Achieving first profitability under the same team suggests mission alignment, though no specific retention data is available. Insider Buying (0/2): Net insider selling of $11M in the last 12 months, including a $1.3M sale by Director Kristen Robinson in January 2026 [4]. No notable insider purchases identified.

infrastructure.label: WEAK
infrastructure.analysis: Mechanically scored on available metrics. FY2026 (ended Jan 2026) delivered record revenue of $307.7M (+26% YoY) but GAAP net loss of $246.9M, which included a $161.4M non-cash warrant revaluation loss [1]. Adjusted EBITDA was positive at $15.5M and free cash flow was $53M. Cash and short-term investments surged 188% YoY to $640M [1]. D/E of 245.4% scores 0.237 (1x weight). ROE of -78.4% is below 0% floor, scores 0.0 (2x weight). Revenue growth YoY of 41.1% (Q4) scores 1.0 (1x weight). Profit margin of -80.2% is below 0% floor, scores 0.0 (1x weight). Forward P/E is negative (excluded). PEG unavailable (excluded). Revenue growth 5yr CAGR unavailable (excluded). Weighted sum: 1.237 / max 5.0 = infrastructure score of 2. Note: GAAP metrics heavily distorted by non-cash warrant liability revaluation; adjusted profitability metrics are significantly better. Liquidity floor passes: avg volume 13.3M x $51.14 = $682M.

ai_resilience.label: LOW RISK
ai_resilience.analysis: AI Risk Protection (7/9): Planet Labs cannot be disrupted by AI — it IS the data source that AI Earth observation models are trained on [2]. The physical satellite constellation (200+ satellites), daily imaging cadence, and proprietary 9-year historical archive create a data moat that AI structurally cannot replicate without actual hardware in orbit. AI increases demand for Planet's data as more AI models require real-world geospatial training data. Physical satellite infrastructure, launch logistics, and orbital operations are entirely beyond AI's capability to substitute. Some risk exists from AI-generated synthetic imagery or competitor AI analytics, but real satellite data remains essential for defense, intelligence, and scientific applications. AI Opportunity (1/1): Planet is generating quantifiable AI-related revenue through AI-powered analytics products — the NVIDIA GPU collaboration, AI chat interface to satellite imagery, crop monitoring, deforestation detection, and maritime tracking [2]. CEO Marshall declared '2026 is the year AI for Planet,' with AI products representing a growing share of platform value.

infinite_game.summary: Planet Labs' decade-long mission to image the entire Earth every day is one of the most genuine Just Causes in the commercial space industry. The company's PBC structure encodes this mission into fiduciary duty — a Sinek-worthy structural commitment to purpose over quarterly earnings. The achievement of profitability after 10 years of investment validates infinite game patience. However, growing dependence on defense and intelligence contracts and net insider selling create some tension with the sustainability-focused founding vision.

just_cause.analysis: Just Cause (2/3): Planet's purpose — 'accelerate humanity toward a more sustainable, secure, and prosperous world, by illuminating environmental and social change' — is genuinely transformative and differentiated [3]. The PBC corporate structure makes this mission a legal fiduciary obligation, not optional marketing. The company has held this cause through 10+ years of losses, SPAC volatility, and technology challenges. However, the growing dominance of defense/intelligence revenue (70%+ growth) creates tension with the sustainability-focused founding vision. The cause is genuine but has not yet been tested through a multi-decade crisis.

trusting_teams.analysis: Trusting Teams (1/2): Co-founder Will Marshall has led Planet from founding through IPO and to first profitability, providing leadership continuity that is rare among SPAC-era companies [2]. The 800-person team has navigated significant financial uncertainty while maintaining operational capability (200+ satellites in orbit). No evidence of major cultural disruptions. Adequate trust, though limited specific data on employee experience or retention.

worthy_rivals.analysis: Worthy Rivals (1/1): Planet explicitly positions against Maxar (high-resolution defense imaging), BlackSky (analytics), and Airbus Defence & Space, using their strengths in resolution and SAR capabilities as motivation to improve its own revisit rate, analytics platform, and AI capabilities [1]. The NVIDIA partnership and AI pivot were directly informed by competitive dynamics in the geospatial intelligence market.

existential_flexibility.analysis: Existential Flexibility (1/2): Planet's pivot from satellite hardware manufacturer to data-intelligence platform is meaningful strategic evolution — recognizing that long-term value lies in the AI analytics layer built on unique daily global data, not in the satellites themselves [1][2]. The expansion into defense/intelligence from a primarily commercial/environmental customer base also represents significant market flexibility. However, these are evolutionary pivots rather than transformative reinventions — the core business remains satellite Earth observation.

courage_to_lead.analysis: Courage to Lead (1/2): Maintaining investment in satellite launches and data infrastructure through 10 years of losses to achieve FY2026 profitability required sustained conviction [1]. The decision to pursue the PBC structure — encoding mission into fiduciary duty — at IPO was a courageous structural choice that limits management's ability to prioritize short-term earnings over mission. However, Planet's $640M cash position now provides significant cushion, making continued investment less risky than during the pre-profitability years.

investment_thesis: Planet Labs hit a fundamental inflection in FY2026 — first full-year adjusted EBITDA profitability ($15.5M), first full-year free cash flow profitability ($53M), and record revenue of $307.7M (+26% YoY) with Q4 accelerating to 41% growth [1]. The $900M backlog (+79% YoY) and FY2027 guidance of $415-440M (+39% YoY) provide strong forward visibility [1]. Cash surged 188% to $640M, the balance sheet is strengthened, and Defense & Intelligence revenue growth of 70%+ YoY demonstrates expanding government adoption of Planet's daily global imagery [1]. CEO Marshall's declaration that '2026 is the year AI for Planet' positions the company's unique 9-year daily Earth imagery archive as a premium data asset in the AI era [2]. The 9-analyst Buy consensus validates the growth trajectory.
thesis_continued: The bear case centers on valuation and GAAP profitability. At $51.14/share and $18.2B market cap, PL trades at a significant premium to FY2027 revenue guidance — an aggressive multiple for a company that is still GAAP unprofitable with a $246.9M net loss in FY2026 (including $161.4M non-cash warrant revaluation) [1]. The average analyst target of $31.33 is well below the current stock price, suggesting the market has priced in significant future growth that may already be reflected [5]. Net insider selling of $11M in the past 12 months signals less than full insider conviction at elevated prices [4]. Government budget concentration risk is meaningful — any reduction in US defense/intelligence satellite data spending could impair the fastest-growing revenue segment. Competition from SAR providers (Capella Space, Umbra) offering all-weather imaging and from Maxar's high-resolution capabilities could erode pricing power over time.
key_catalysts: FY2027 revenue reaching $415-440M guidance range (+39% YoY) would confirm the AI data-intelligence business model is working at scale and support the premium valuation multiple [1]
First full-year GAAP profitability removing the last financial overhang and unlocking institutional capital from mandates requiring profitable companies [1]

Major new multi-year government intelligence contract (NGA, Navy, or allied nation) at $100M+ value demonstrating Planet's transition from tactical data supplier to strategic intelligence infrastructure partner [1][5]
key_risks: Valuation risk — at $51.14/share, the stock is 63% above the $31.33 average analyst target, implying the market has front-run the growth story; any miss on FY2027 $415-440M guidance could trigger significant multiple compression [5]

Government budget concentration — Defense & Intelligence is the fastest-growing segment at 70%+ YoY; any reduction in US or allied defense satellite intelligence budgets would disproportionately impact revenue trajectory [1]

GAAP profitability remains elusive — FY2026 net loss of $246.9M (including $161.4M non-cash warrant revaluation) means the company cannot yet demonstrate sustainable profitability on standard accounting metrics [1]
